metadata.description: Challenges validating resilience patterns including circuit breakers, timeouts, retries, graceful degradation, and connection pool recovery

challenges.name: Circuit Breaker Activation and Recovery
challenges.description: Verify that the AdaptiveRateLimiter circuit breaker opens after consecutive failures exceed the threshold, rejects requests while open, and transitions back to closed after a cooldown period. Test with 1000 rapid failing calls followed by a recovery period.

outputs.name: circuit_opened
outputs.description: Whether the circuit breaker opened after failures

outputs.name: requests_rejected_while_open
outputs.description: Number of requests rejected while circuit is open

outputs.name: circuit_recovered
outputs.description: Whether the circuit breaker recovered after cooldown

outputs.name: recovery_time_ms
outputs.description: Time taken for circuit breaker to recover

assertions.message: Circuit breaker must open after consecutive failures
At least one request must be rejected while circuit is open
Circuit breaker must recover after cooldown period

challenges.name: Timeout Handling Across Protocols
challenges.description: Verify that all network protocol services (Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, FTP, WebDAV) correctly handle connection timeouts and request timeouts. Each protocol must respect configured timeout values and throw appropriate timeout exceptions rather than hanging indefinitely.

outputs.name: all_protocols_respect_timeout
outputs.description: Whether all protocols respected the configured timeout

outputs.name: hanging_protocols
outputs.description: Protocols that hung beyond the configured timeout

assertions.message: All protocols must respect configured timeouts
No protocol should hang beyond its configured timeout
All timeout results must indicate proper timeout handling

challenges.name: Retry Behavior with Exponential Backoff
challenges.description: Verify that the OperationThrottler correctly implements retry logic with exponential backoff. Test that retries occur at increasing intervals, respect the maximum retry count, and eventually succeed when the underlying operation recovers.

outputs.name: retry_count
outputs.description: Number of retries attempted before success

outputs.name: retry_intervals_ms
outputs.description: Actual delay between each retry attempt

outputs.name: backoff_pattern_correct
outputs.description: Whether retry intervals follow exponential backoff

outputs.name: operation_succeeded
outputs.description: Whether the operation eventually succeeded after retries

assertions.message: At least one retry must be attempted
Retry intervals must follow exponential backoff pattern
Operation must eventually succeed after retries

challenges.name: Graceful Degradation Under Load
challenges.description: Verify that the system degrades gracefully when overwhelmed with concurrent parsing requests. Under load of 500 concurrent parse operations, the system should complete all operations (possibly with increased latency) rather than crashing, deadlocking, or producing corrupted results.

outputs.name: avg_latency_ms
outputs.description: Average latency per operation under load

outputs.name: system_stable
outputs.description: Whether the system remained stable throughout the test

assertions.message: At least 90% of operations must complete successfully
System must remain stable under load
Average latency must remain under 5 seconds even under load

challenges.name: Connection Pool Exhaustion Recovery
challenges.description: Verify that the system recovers from connection pool exhaustion. Saturate the connection pool by opening maximum connections, then verify that new requests queue or fail gracefully rather than crashing. After connections are released, verify that queued requests are served.

outputs.name: pool_saturated
outputs.description: Whether the connection pool was successfully saturated

outputs.name: overflow_handled_gracefully
outputs.description: Whether overflow requests were handled gracefully

outputs.name: recovery_after_release
outputs.description: Whether queued requests were served after connections released

assertions.message: Overflow requests must be handled gracefully
System must recover after connections are released
